evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 9 Questions but page shows ~16 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice 4/10 — Reads like a committee-produced SEO template with editorial byline and formulaic structure.
Genuine opinions 6/10 — Has a few real takes (Duke's is touristy but worth it, Pig and the Lady non-negotiable) but mostly safe recommendations.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Some decent phrases ('who's on aux', 'war zone') but mostly predictable travel-blog cadence.
Quotable sentences 5/10 — A couple of lines have snap ('who's on aux'), but most sentences are functional not memorable.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — Buried under duplicated title, metadata, and TL;DR before the actual hook lands.

answer_extractability 5/10 — The primary keyword answer is fragmented across multiple sections and buried beneath structural headers like 'What Is Quick answer?' and 'What Is Key takeaways?'. No single 40–60 word self-contained paragraph exists that an AI could lift cleanly. The day-by-day answer is accurate but split across seven separate entries with no synthesizing paragraph an extractor could grab as a standalone block. The TL;DR is close but splits across two sentences and omits Days 3–7 entirely.
